title: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) Programme Overview
text: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) 2025 Program Overview Award: BSc (Hons) Duration: 3 years Course Type: Full Time Start Date: 08 September 2025 Choosing this programme for 2025 This is a prestigious degree that offers flexible and versatile career planning and professional certification. The degree is mapped to the latest developments in financial reporting, from sustainability reporting through to international reporting standards. The programme is taught in English, and content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree delivered to University of Southampton students in other locations. We expect that this programme will:- Take students towards a wide range of professional goals, while conveniently completing their undergraduate degree. Offer a window into International Financial Reporting Standards that are closely aligned to UK Financial Reporting Standards for a global picture, while being contextualised to India. Emphasise highly desirable and employability boosting skills compared to other recent graduates. Provide valuable accreditations that entitle students to earn specific exam exemptions for professional bodies including:- CIMA (Chartered Institute of Management Accountants) CIPFA (Chartered Institute of Public Finance and Accountancy) AACSB (Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business) We are currently applying for exemptions for ACCA (Association of Chartered Certified Accountants) and AIA (Association of International Accountants). Once approved, eligible students may receive exemptions from certain ACCA and AIA exams.
summary: The BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance 2025 program offers flexible career planning, professional certification, and international reporting standards alignment, contextualized for India.

title: Unique Features and Student Benefits of the Programme
text: What makes the programme unique? The School of Business is the only business school in Europe that holds a licence to impart training in Sustainability Reporting. Includes opportunities for gaining practical experience through client projects and activities with professional bodies. Allows students to keep pace with preparations for professional exams. As a BSc degree, it signals analytical rigour and is a strong asset in the employment market. Which students will benefit the most? Students who want flexibility to select their best-fit professional option will benefit from the programme. Students can work towards the requirements of global firms in terms of international reporting standards, professional preparation and certification, or research. The structure of the programme allows students to shape the degree towards their objectives.
summary: This program offers unique features like Sustainability Reporting training, practical experience opportunities, and prepares students for global firm requirements or research.

title: BSc Accounting and Finance Course Structure and General Modules
text: What you will study These course modules provide examples of what you can expect to learn on this degree programme, based on recent academic teaching. As a research-led university, we review our programmes continuously to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. Modules with a difference Sustainability Reporting: Students work with a small and medium enterprise to prepare a sustainability report in terms of the Global Reporting initiative. The Sustainability Reporting course earns students a certification. Programme modules are aligned to accreditation norms: The course covers foundational concepts in year 1. From year 2, the programme is designed to support preparation either for professional qualification through subject exemptions for exams conducted by professional bodies, or for management roles in finance, or for entrepreneurship selecting of different combinations of core, compulsory and optional modules. The research dissertation module in year 3 is key to the development of critical thinking. Every student uniformly acquires a research background in the undergraduate degree, emerging as an independent thinker with an Honours degree in three years. The precise course modules available to you in future years may vary depending on staff availability and research interests, new study topics, timetabling, and student demand. Find out why, when and how we might make changes.
summary: The program structure combines foundational concepts in Year 1 with specialization options in later years, including a research dissertation and sustainability reporting certification.

title: Detailed Course Modules (Years 1-3)
text: Course modules Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. Optional modules may include modules related to the core discipline as well as options built around contemporary, real-world trends that change from time to time. Find out why, when and how we might make changes. In Year 1, students will take 8 fixed course modules. In Year 2, students will take 7 fixed course modules, and 1 optional elective. In Year 3, students will take 3 fixed course modules, and 4 optional electives. In Year 1, students must take all these course modules:- Commercial Law MANG1014 Financial Accounting 1 MANG1044 Introduction to Accounting and Finance MANG1046 Introduction to Economics For Non-Economists ECON1009 Introduction to Management MANG1003 Introduction to Marketing MANG1015 Management Accounting 1 MANG1045 Management Analysis MANG1047 In Year 2, students must take all these course modules:- Company Law MANG2017 Contemporary Issues in Accounting MANG2098 Financial Accounting 2 MANG2003 Financial Management MANG2015 Management Accounting 2 MANG2005 Portfolio Theory and Financial Markets MANG2004 Principles of Audit and Taxation MANG2066 In Year 2, students must choose one of these optional electives:- Human Resource Management MANG2011 Management Ethics MANG2041 In Year 3, students must take all these course modules:- Accounting Research Project MANG3119 Financial Accounting 3 MANG3003 Management Accounting 3 MANG3006 In Year 3, students must choose two of these optional electives:- Advanced Taxation MANG3076 Corporate Governance MANG3021 Financial Analysis, Information and Markets MANG3030 Futures and Options MANG3020 Strategic Management MANG3008 In Year 3, students must choose two of these optional electives:- Auditing MANG3015 Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainable Business MANG3029 International Banking MANG3009 Project Management MANG3034
summary: The Accounting and Finance program outlines course modules across three years, including core subjects like Commercial Law and Financial Management, and optional electives in specialized areas such as Advanced Taxation and Project Management.
entities: Corporate Social Responsibility and Sustainable Business

title: Career Prospects and Employability Services
text: Your future career With an accounting and finance degree from a double-world top 100 university, you’ll have versatile analytical and problem-solving skills for many professional roles within the sector. Career opportunities You could choose to become: an auditor a tax specialist a management accountant a consultant Companies you could work at University of Southampton graduates have secured roles in organisations including: Bank of China Barclays Capital Deloitte Development Bank of Singapore Deutsche Bank Ernst & Young (EY) PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) Like all our business school programmes, this degree is also a good foundation for further study at postgraduate level. Careers services at University of Southampton Delhi As well as support from our team in Delhi, you’ll have access to our market-leading employability development offering. This includes: online resources regular workshops one-to-one guidance and support with qualified careers professionals through our MyCareer platform Through our e-mentoring scheme, you’ll also have access to: tailored career planning advice on employability skills industry insight We have incorporated our employability skills model, Successful Futures, into our curriculum. Our links with multi-national and industrial organisations mean that you can expect to engage with employers and explore potential career paths. You will receive a personalised employability programme that will provide excellent prospects for securing a premium placement and financial security on graduating. We also host regular alumni events hosted in person across major cities in India, allowing you to grow your own networks. Our support is ongoing, and once you graduate, you’ll be part of a global community of over 220,000 alumni that spans over 180 countries.
summary: Graduates of the Accounting and Finance degree are prepared for diverse professional roles in global firms, supported by University of Southampton Delhi’s comprehensive career services, e-mentoring, and alumni networking opportunities.

title: Admission Eligibility and English Language Requirements
text: Key information To help plan your year we have published University of Southampton Delhi term and holiday dates in advance, but these may be subject to change. We expect to provide 25 seats on this programme for academic year 25/26. Eligibility criteria Students need not have studied accountancy before; prior knowledge of accountancy is helpful but not essential. Part 2 modules take students through the basics of economics, accounting and finance, and marketing to ensure that all students fully benefit from the programme. For Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): Minimum 78% in your India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary School Examination (Standard X) examination For Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE): Minimum 78% in your Indian School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) examination (overall) and minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (Standard X) examination For State Board: Minimum 83% in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination (overall) and Minimum 75% in Mathematics in your Senior Secondary School Certificate (Standard X) examination International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher Level and 4 points in Maths and English at Standard level A Levels: AAB, plus Grade B or above in GCSE Maths English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve been taught and assessed in English, including at a CBSE or CISCE school, for your final 4 years of high school, and have achieved at least 70% in English Language, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.
summary: Eligibility for the Accounting and Finance program requires specific academic percentages from CBSE, CICSE, State Board, IB, or A Levels, and meeting English language proficiency via IELTS or school assessment.
entities: Council Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (CICSE)

title: Tuition Fees and Payment Structure for 2025
text: Our fees You can pay your tuition fee in up to 4 instalments. The fees for a year’s study, starting in August 2025, are: 13,20,000 INR for India students 15,40,000 INR for Overseas students Your first payment will be a deposit of 10% of the programme fee. To confirm your offer, you must pay your deposit within 28 days of receiving a conditional offer, and no later than 14 days before the start of term. For students starting in August 2025, the deposit is: 132,000 INR for India students 154,000 INR for Overseas students You’ll then pay the balance of your yearly tuition fee as you study, in up to 3 instalments. If you’re an India student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 316,800 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 435,600 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 435,600 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 If you’re an Overseas student, you’ll have to make 3 separate payments of: 369,600 INR by Monday 8 September 2025 508,200 INR by Monday 26 January 2026 508,200 INR by Monday 20 April 2026 We may adjust the fees in the future to account for inflation and changes in other costs. Exact payment dates for future years will vary according to the academic calendar.
summary: Tuition fees for the 2025 academic year are 13,20,000 INR for India students and 15,40,000 INR for overseas students, payable in a 10% deposit and up to three subsequent instalments.

title: Scholarships, Funding, and Application Offers FAQ
text: Funding Scholarships We offer competitive scholarships to academically gifted students and talented students who would benefit from study financially through a merit bursary. Funding support Please email us about the partnerships we have developed with trusted and reputable financial institutions keen to support University of Southampton Delhi students with a range of education loans. You might also want to know Can I apply for a scholarship or bursary? We welcome applications for a scholarship or bursary from any self-funding student who will be paying domestic Tuition Fees. Additional criteria, such as programme eligibility, are shown clearly on individual award pages. You must first make an application to study a specific programme before you make an application for a scholarship or bursary. Can I get an offer with predicted scores? We encourage students to apply as soon as possible so that they can receive a Conditional Offer based on predicted scores for their chosen programme. We can accept an application from a student using their predicted grades if we receive their final grades once they are available.
summary: University of Southampton Delhi offers competitive scholarships and education loan partnerships, with FAQs detailing eligibility for self-funding students and the process for receiving conditional offers based on predicted scores.

title: General Application Process and Programme Document
text: Our application process Admissions are now open for a place at our campus in the Delhi NCR. Our first cohort for undergraduate bachelor’s degrees starts in August 2025. Potential students can understand more about our application process by looking at helpful guidance and advice on university admissions pages. Download more details This downloadable document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, the programme structure, information on course modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. PDF BSc Hons Accounting and Finance 2025 Programme Description Document 99 KB How to apply Our programmes are taught in English, and content is equivalent to that delivered to other University of Southampton students. To match the programme lengths of internationally respected UK awarded degrees, undergraduates study for three years, and postgraduates study for one year.
summary: Admissions are open for undergraduate degrees at University of Southampton Delhi NCR, providing guidance on the application process and a downloadable programme description document, with programmes mirroring UK standards.
entities: BSc Hons Accounting and Finance 2025 Programme Description Document

title: Honours Degree Structure and Benefits FAQ
text: Why is an Honours degree good for me? An Honours degree, shown with (Hons) in the programme title, mean that students will understand the fundamentals of their discipline and then build specialisation and perspective through optional modules. These fast-paced, intense programmes deliver essential learning in the first academic year and offer optional course modules, or electives, in the second year. Honours degrees are offered at University of Southampton campuses across the world, reflecting teaching and learning to a higher academic standard, meaning that an Honours degree would hold more value post graduation for future study and career development. Why is my degree award BSc (Hons) or BEng (Hons)? Degrees with a BA and BSc award do not represent a stream, but a broad categorisation that reflects student choice and leaning. Our BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons) undergraduate degrees are oriented towards specialist, technical roles in industry with an analytical approach, not science subjects. How can I earn an Honours degree in just 3 years? The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally. What is an option? Our programmes are designed to make sure that students quickly understand the fundamentals of their discipline, and then build specialisation and perspective through electives, or optional, modules. These optional modules are typically offered in academic years 2 and 3, adding depth and breadth to core learning from previous years. As these options are often built around real-world trends, the specific choice of module available to students from year to year may vary.
summary: This FAQ explains the benefits of an Honours degree, differentiates BSc (Hons) and BEng (Hons), and clarifies the structure of the 3-year undergraduate degrees and the role of optional modules at University of Southampton Delhi.
